turns.content: Good morning, and welcome to the Royal Bank of Canada Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered strong results in Q1 2024, demonstrating the power of our diversified business model. Total revenue reached $14.8 billion, with net income of $3.9 billion. Our ROE of 15.2% reflects disciplined capital management and strong execution. We saw strong performance in Capital Markets with trading revenue up 18%. We saw successful integration of HSBC Canada continues ahead of schedule. Our focus on HSBC Canada integration and technology investment is delivering measurable results. Technology remains a strategic priority. We are investing aggressively in AI, machine learning, and cloud infrastructure. Our Quantitative Technology Services group continues to expand, and we are seeing real impact from these investments in trading efficiency, risk management, and client experience. Our Halifax technology hub is a key part of this strategy, bringing together top engineering talent to build next-generation platforms. We remain confident in our strategic direction and our ability to deliver consistent, sustainable growth. Our technology investments are creating durable competitive advantages that will drive value for shareholders over the long term.

Thank you, Dave. Let me walk through the financial details for Q1 2024. Total revenue was $14.8 billion, up from the prior quarter. Net income of $3.9 billion reflects an ROE of 15.2%. Our CET1 capital ratio was 14.1%, well above regulatory minimums and providing significant flexibility for organic growth and capital returns. Provisions for credit losses were $813 million, declining as credit quality improves. In Capital Markets, revenue was solid, driven by global markets trading and investment banking advisory fees. Wealth Management AUM growth was supported by positive net new asset flows and market appreciation. Technology spending represented approximately 10% of revenue, reflecting our commitment to building scalable, AI-driven platforms.

Good morning. On the HSBC Canada integration front, can you give us more detail on how this is impacting the business operationally? Are you seeing measurable efficiency gains from the AI and technology investments?

Thanks, Gabriel. On HSBC Canada integration, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 14.1% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $813 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.

Hi, thanks for taking my question. I wanted to ask about credit quality and how you are thinking about provisions going forward given the macro outlook. Also any color on the HSBC Canada integration initiative would be helpful.

Thanks, Sohrab. On HSBC Canada integration, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 14.1% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $813 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.

Good morning. On the Capital Markets strength front, can you give us more detail on how this is impacting the business operationally? Are you seeing measurable efficiency gains from the AI and technology investments?

Thanks, John. On Capital Markets strength, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 14.1% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $813 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.
This concludes the question and answer session. I will turn the call back to management.

Thank you all for joining us today. We are proud of these results and excited about the opportunities ahead. Our investments in technology and talent are creating a stronger, more innovative Royal Bank of Canada. Have a great day.

Good morning, and welcome to the Royal Bank of Canada Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am very pleased to report an outstanding Q2 2024 for Royal Bank of Canada. Total revenue reached $15.1 billion, with net income of $4.1 billion. Our ROE of 16.0% reflects disciplined capital management and strong execution. We saw record revenue quarter driven by Capital Markets and Wealth Management. We saw HSBC Canada integration milestones achieved in retail branch conversion. Our focus on AI and digital transformation and HSBC synergies is delivering measurable results. Technology remains a strategic priority. We are investing aggressively in AI, machine learning, and cloud infrastructure. Our Quantitative Technology Services group continues to expand, and we are seeing real impact from these investments in trading efficiency, risk management, and client experience. Our Halifax technology hub is a key part of this strategy, bringing together top engineering talent to build next-generation platforms. Looking ahead, we see significant runway for continued growth. Our investments in technology, particularly in AI and our Quantitative Technology Services platform, position us exceptionally well for the future. We are raising our medium-term targets to reflect this confidence.

Thank you, Dave. Let me walk through the financial details for Q2 2024. Total revenue was $15.1 billion, up from the prior quarter. Net income of $4.1 billion reflects an ROE of 16.0%. Our CET1 capital ratio was 13.8%, well above regulatory minimums and providing significant flexibility for organic growth and capital returns. Provisions for credit losses were $920 million, reflecting our prudent approach to the current credit environment. In Capital Markets, revenue was solid, driven by global markets trading and investment banking advisory fees. Wealth Management AUM growth was supported by positive net new asset flows and market appreciation. Technology spending represented approximately 10% of revenue, reflecting our commitment to building scalable, AI-driven platforms.

Good morning. On the record results front, can you give us more detail on how this is impacting the business operationally? Are you seeing measurable efficiency gains from the AI and technology investments?

Thanks, Ebrahim. On record results, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 13.8% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $920 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.

Hi, thanks for taking my question. I wanted to ask about credit quality and how you are thinking about provisions going forward given the macro outlook. Also any color on the HSBC synergies initiative would be helpful.

Thanks, John. On HSBC synergies, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 13.8% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $920 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.

Thank you. Dave, can you talk more about the technology investment strategy, particularly around AI and digital transformation? How should we think about the return on these investments over the next two to three years?

Great question, Sohrab. AI and digital transformation is central to our strategy. We are building a technology-first bank, and the investments we are making today in AI, cloud, and our QTS platform are creating capabilities that will differentiate us for years to come. Our Halifax technology hub is a great example. We are attracting exceptional engineering talent and they are building systems that improve everything from algorithmic trading to fraud detection to client personalization. The ROI on these investments is accelerating. We are seeing tangible efficiency gains in operations, better risk-adjusted returns in trading, and stronger client engagement through digital channels.
This concludes the question and answer session. I will turn the call back to management.

Thank you all for joining us today. We are proud of these results and excited about the opportunities ahead. Our investments in technology and talent are creating a stronger, more innovative Royal Bank of Canada. Have a great day.

Good morning, and welcome to the Royal Bank of Canada Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results in Q3 2024, navigating a complex operating environment effectively. Total revenue reached $14.5 billion, with net income of $3.6 billion. Our ROE of 14.8% reflects disciplined capital management and strong execution. We saw solid results despite elevated provisions for credit losses. We saw Capital Markets revenue normalized from prior quarter highs. Our focus on credit quality vigilance and technology modernization is delivering measurable results. Technology remains a strategic priority. We are investing aggressively in AI, machine learning, and cloud infrastructure. Our Quantitative Technology Services group continues to expand, and we are seeing real impact from these investments in trading efficiency, risk management, and client experience. Our Halifax technology hub is a key part of this strategy, bringing together top engineering talent to build next-generation platforms. While we remain watchful of macro conditions, our diversified model provides resilience. We continue to invest in technology and talent to strengthen our competitive position and are well-prepared for a range of economic scenarios.

Thank you, Dave. Let me walk through the financial details for Q3 2024. Total revenue was $14.5 billion, consistent with from the prior quarter. Net income of $3.6 billion reflects an ROE of 14.8%. Our CET1 capital ratio was 13.5%, well above regulatory minimums and providing significant flexibility for organic growth and capital returns. Provisions for credit losses were $1050 million, reflecting our prudent approach to the current credit environment. In Capital Markets, revenue was solid, driven by global markets trading and investment banking advisory fees. Wealth Management AUM growth was supported by positive net new asset flows and market appreciation. Technology spending represented approximately 10% of revenue, reflecting our commitment to building scalable, AI-driven platforms.

Thank you. Dave, can you talk more about the technology investment strategy, particularly around housing market? How should we think about the return on these investments over the next two to three years?

Thanks, Meny. On housing market, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 13.5% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $1050 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.

Good morning. On the housing market front, can you give us more detail on how this is impacting the business operationally? Are you seeing measurable efficiency gains from the AI and technology investments?

Thanks, John. On housing market, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 13.5% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $1050 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.

Hi, thanks for taking my question. I wanted to ask about credit quality and how you are thinking about provisions going forward given the macro outlook. Also any color on the credit quality vigilance initiative would be helpful.

Thanks, Sohrab. On credit quality vigilance, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 13.5% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $1050 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.
This concludes the question and answer session. I will turn the call back to management.

Thank you all for joining us today. We are proud of these results and excited about the opportunities ahead. Our investments in technology and talent are creating a stronger, more innovative Royal Bank of Canada. Have a great day.

Good morning, and welcome to the Royal Bank of Canada Q1 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am very pleased to report an outstanding Q1 2025 for Royal Bank of Canada. Total revenue reached $16.2 billion, with net income of $4.6 billion. Our ROE of 17.1% reflects disciplined capital management and strong execution. We saw strong start to fiscal 2025 with double-digit earnings growth. We saw Capital Markets had its best Q1 in five years. Our focus on earnings momentum and technology acceleration is delivering measurable results. Technology remains a strategic priority. In Capital Markets, revenue exceeded expectations, driven by global markets trading and investment banking advisory fees. Wealth Management AUM growth was supported by positive net new asset flows and market appreciation. Technology spending represented approximately 10% of revenue, reflecting our commitment to building scalable, AI-driven platforms.

Thanks, Gabriel. On earnings momentum, the numbers reflect the underlying strength of our franchise. Our CET1 ratio of 14.2% gives us significant flexibility. Provisions at $780 million reflect our disciplined approach to risk management. We continue to see strong fundamentals in our Canadian personal banking portfolio and are well-positioned regardless of the rate environment.

Good morning. On the technology acceleration front, can you give us more detail on how this is impacting the business operationally? Are you seeing measurable efficiency gains from the AI and technology investments?

Great question, Meny. technology acceleration is central to our strategy. We are building a technology-first bank, and the investments we are making today in AI, cloud, and our QTS platform are creating capabilities that will differentiate us for years to come. Our Halifax technology hub is a great example.
